scope: Backend Salesforce round-trip reduction only. Frontend, API URLs, response schemas, auth, RBAC, FLS, sharing, reports, dashboards, admin, layouts, and business behavior were preserved.

capabilities: Automatic fallback to the existing REST implementation

integrationPoints.behavior: Fetches record and object describe through one Composite request when describe metadata is not already cached. If describe metadata is cached, existing one-record REST path remains.
Batches multiple lookup-label SOQL queries into one Composite request when a record references multiple object types.
Batches EmailMessage, Task, and Event read queries into one Composite request with per-query fallback warnings preserved.
Adds admin-only /api/debug/composite and /api/debug/composite/reset endpoints for composite counters.

recordDetailColdDescribe.notes: Record + describe are composite-batched only when metadata is cold. Warm metadata remains one record request.

lookupLabels.before: 1 Salesforce query per referenced object type
lookupLabels.after: 1 Composite request for all referenced object-type label queries when more than one group exists

activityPanel.notes: EmailMessage, Task, and Event queries are safe independent reads.

beforeAfterLatency.expectedImpact: Latency improvement is proportional to removed Salesforce HTTP round trips. The largest expected win is Activity, followed by cold record detail and multi-object lookup label hydration.

liveTiming.reason: Static validation was performed in this environment. Runtime counters are now available at /api/debug/composite for admin users.

details: Composite is skipped for zero or one eligible subrequest.
If Composite API fails or returns any non-2xx subresponse, the client falls back to each subrequest's existing REST implementation.
Fallbacks reuse the existing sfGet path, including auth refresh retry and perf instrumentation.
User-visible responses preserve the same shape because only backend Salesforce transport changed.

remainingBottlenecks: Related list queries still have opportunities for deeper batching, but relationship-specific fallbacks should be covered by parity tests before expanding.
Large SOQL result payload parsing is unchanged.
Composite Graph remains a prepared foundation and was not enabled in Sprint 11.
